mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show negative mean when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.5% signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing investors to price in negative earnings revisions and driving S&P 500 returns negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >55% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 9.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, forcing equity models to upgrade near-term earnings forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day returns in >60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates accelerating factory output and supply chain normalization, which triggers short-term algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases of >0.5% are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in >50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price indices combined with artificially low policy rates compress real yields and signal monetary policy misalignment, prompting portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition of CPIAUCSL above 210 and FEDFUNDS below 0.5% yields positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding corporate hiring and wage growth, which directly supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with 20-day forward returns, producing a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP falling below 12800 billion chained dollars indicates contracting aggregate demand and weakening corporate revenue pipelines, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 readings below 12800 are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in >60% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production and payroll employment confirms synchronized real economy expansion, triggering short-term momentum flows and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Synchronized positive shocks to INDPRO and PAYEMS result in negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in >50% of triggered periods.
